14.0.Title: Equality before law
14.0.Description: ["The State shall not deny to any person equality before the law or the equal protection of the laws within India."]
14.0.Chunk Text: ["The State shall not deny to any person equality before the law or the equal protection of the laws within India."]

15.0.Title: Prohibition of discrimination on grounds of religion, race, caste, sex or place of birth
15.0.Description: ["(1) The State shall not discriminate against any citizen on grounds only of religion, race, caste, sex, place of birth, or any of them. (", "2) No citizen shall be subject to any disability, liability, restriction, or condition with regard to access to shops, public restaurants, hotels, and places of public entertainment, or the use of wells, tanks, bathing ghats, roads, and places of public resort maintained wholly or partly out of State funds or dedicated to the use of the general public, on grounds only of religion, race, caste, sex, place of birth, or any of them."]
15.0.Chunk Text: ["(1) The State shall not discriminate against any citizen on grounds only of religion, race, caste, sex, place of birth, or any of them.(2) No citizen shall be subject to any disability, liability, restriction, or condition with regard to access to shops, public restaurants, hotels, and places of public entertainment, or the use of wells, tanks, bathing ghats, roads, and places of public resort maintained wholly or partly out of State funds or dedicated to the use of the general public, on grounds only of religion, race, caste, sex, place of birth, or any of them."]

16.0.Title: Equality of opportunity in matters of public employment
16.0.Description: ["(1) All citizens shall have equal opportunities in matters relating to employment or appointment to any office under the State. (", "2) No citizen shall be ineligible for or discriminated against in any employment or office under the State based solely on their religion, race, caste, sex, descent, place of birth, residence, or any of these factors. (", "3) This article does not prevent Parliament from making laws requiring a class or classes of employment or appointment to an office (under the Government of, or any local or other authority within, a State or Union territory) to reside within that State or Union territory prior to such employment or appointment. (", "4) This article does not prevent the State from making provisions for the reservation of appointments or posts in favour of any backward class of citizens that, in the State\u2019s opinion, is not adequately represented in the services under the State."]
16.0.Chunk Text: ["(1) All citizens shall have equal opportunities in matters relating to employment or appointment to any office under the State. (", "2) No citizen shall be ineligible for or discriminated against in any employment or office under the State based solely on their religion, race, caste, sex, descent, place of birth, residence, or any of these factors.(3) This article does not prevent Parliament from making laws requiring a class or classes of employment or appointment to an office (under the Government of, or any local or other authority within, a State or Union territory) to reside within that State or Union territory prior to such employment or appointment.(4) This article does not prevent the State from making provisions for the reservation of appointments or posts in favour of any backward class of citizens that, in the State\u2019s opinion, is not adequately represented in the services under the State."]

17.0.Title: Abolition of Untouchability
17.0.Description: ["Untouchability is abolished, and its practice in any form is forbidden. The enforcement of any disability arising out of \"Untouchability\" shall be an offence punishable by law."]
17.0.Chunk Text: ["Untouchability is abolished, and its practice in any form is forbidden. The enforcement of any disability arising out of \"Untouchability\" shall be an offence punishable by law."]

18.0.Title: Abolition of titles
18.0.Description: ["(1) No title shall be conferred by the State, not being a military or academic distinction. ( 2) No citizen of India shall accept any title from any foreign State. (", "3) No person who is not a citizen of India shall accept any title from any foreign State while holding any office of profit or trust under the State, without the consent of the President. (", "4) No person holding any office of profit or trust under the State shall accept any present, emolument, or office of any kind from or under any foreign State, without the consent of the President."]
18.0.Chunk Text: ["(1) No title shall be conferred by the State, not being a military or academic distinction. (", "2) No citizen of India shall accept any title from any foreign State.(3) No person who is not a citizen of India shall accept any title from any foreign State while holding any office of profit or trust under the State, without the consent of the President. (", "4) No person holding any office of profit or trust under the State shall accept any present, emolument, or office of any kind from or under any foreign State, without the consent of the President."]

19.0.Title: Protection of certain rights regarding freedom of speech
19.0.Description: ["All citizens shall have the following rights: - Freedom of speech and expression - The right to assemble peacefully and without arms - The right to form associations or unions (or co-operative societies) - The right to move freely throughout the territory of India"]
19.0.Chunk Text: ["All citizens shall have the following rights: - Freedom of speech and expression - The right to assemble peacefully and without arms - The right to form associations or unions (or co-operative societies) - The right to move freely throughout the territory of India"]

20.0.Title: Protection in respect of conviction for offences
20.0.Description: ["No person shall be convicted of any offence except for a violation of a law in force at the time of the offence.", "They shall also not be subjected to a penalty greater than that which might have been inflicted under the law in force at the time of the offence.", "Furthermore, no person shall be prosecuted and punished for the same offence more than once. Lastly, no person accused of any offence shall be compelled to be a witness against themselves."]
20.0.Chunk Text: ["No person shall be convicted of any offence except for a violation of a law in force at the time of the offence.", "They shall also not be subjected to a penalty greater than that which might have been inflicted under the law in force at the time of the offence.", "Furthermore, no person shall be prosecuted and punished for the same offence more than once. Lastly, no person accused of any offence shall be compelled to be a witness against themselves."]

21.0.Title: Protection of life and personal liberty
21.0.Description: ["No person shall be deprived of their life or personal liberty except according to procedure established by law."]
21.0.Chunk Text: ["No person shall be deprived of their life or personal liberty except according to procedure established by law."]

21.0.Title: Right to education
21.0.Description: ["The State shall provide free and compulsory education to all children aged six to fourteen years, as determined by law."]
21.0.Chunk Text: ["The State shall provide free and compulsory education to all children aged six to fourteen years, as determined by law."]

22.0.Title: Protection against arrest and detention in certain cases.
22.0.Description: ["(1) No person arrested shall be detained without being informed of the arrest\u2019s grounds and the right to legal counsel. (", "2) Arrested individuals must be produced before a magistrate within 24 hours, excluding travel time. They cannot be detained beyond this period without magistrate authority. (", "3) Clauses (1) and (2) do not apply to enemies of the state or those detained under preventive detention laws. (", "4) Preventive detention laws cannot detain individuals longer than 3 months unless an Advisory Board of High Court judges reports sufficient cause within that period.", "This does not exceed the maximum period set by any law made by Parliament under sub-clause (b) of clause (7), or if detained under sub-clauses (a) and (b) of clause (7). (", "5) When detained under preventive detention laws, the authority must inform the person of the order\u2019s grounds and allow them to make a representation. (", "6) The authority making such an order cannot disclose facts deemed against the public interest."]
22.0.Chunk Text: ["(1) No person arrested shall be detained without being informed of the arrest\u2019s grounds and the right to legal counsel. (", "2) Arrested individuals must be produced before a magistrate within 24 hours, excluding travel time.", "They cannot be detained beyond this period without magistrate authority.(3) Clauses (1) and (2) do not apply to enemies of the state or those detained under preventive detention laws.(4) Preventive detention laws cannot detain individuals longer than 3 months unless an Advisory Board of High Court judges reports sufficient cause within that period.", "This does not exceed the maximum period set by any law made by Parliament under sub-clause (b) of clause (7), or if detained under sub-clauses (a) and (b) of clause (7).(5) When detained under preventive detention laws, the authority must inform the person of the order\u2019s grounds and allow them to make a representation. (", "6) The authority making such an order cannot disclose facts deemed against the public interest."]

23.0.Title: Prohibition of traffic in human beings and forced labour
23.0.Description: ["Human trafficking and forced labour are prohibited and punishable by law. The state can impose compulsory service for public purposes without discrimination."]
23.0.Chunk Text: ["Human trafficking and forced labour are prohibited and punishable by law. The state can impose compulsory service for public purposes without discrimination."]

24.0.Title: Prohibition of employment of children in factories
24.0.Description: ["No child under the age of fourteen years shall be employed in any factory, mine, or other hazardous employment."]
24.0.Chunk Text: ["No child under the age of fourteen years shall be employed in any factory, mine, or other hazardous employment."]

25.0.Title: Freedom of conscience and free profession, practice and propagation of religion
25.0.Description: ["(1) Subject to public order, morality and health and to the other provisions of this Part, all persons are equally entitled to freedom of conscience and the right freely to profess, practice and propagate religion. (", "2) Nothing in this article shall affect the operation of any existing law or prevent the State from making any law\u2014 (a) regulating or restricting any economic, financial, political or other secular activity which may be associated with religious practice; (b) providing for social welfare and reform or the opening of Hindu religious institutions of a public character to all classes and sections of Hindus.", "Explanation I.\u2014The wearing and carrying of kirpans shall be deemed to be included in the profession of the Sikh religion.", "Explanation II.\u2014In sub-clause (b) of clause (2), the reference to Hindus shall be construed as including a reference to persons professing the Sikh, Jaina or Buddhist religion and the reference to Hindu religious institutions shall be construed accordingly."]
25.0.Chunk Text: ["(1) Subject to public order, morality and health and to the other provisions of this Part, all persons are equally entitled to freedom of conscience and the right freely to profess, practice and propagate religion.(2) Nothing in this article shall affect the operation of any existing law or prevent the State from making any law\u2014 (a) regulating or restricting any economic, financial, political or other secular activity which may be associated with religious practice; (b) providing for social welfare and reform or the opening of Hindu religious institutions of a public character to all classes and sections of Hindus.", "Explanation I.\u2014The wearing and carrying of kirpans shall be deemed to be included in the profession of the Sikh religion.", "Explanation II.\u2014In sub-clause (b) of clause (2), the reference to Hindus shall be construed as including a reference to persons professing the Sikh, Jaina or Buddhist religion and the reference to Hindu religious institutions shall be construed accordingly."]

26.0.Title: Freedom to manage religious affairs
26.0.Description: ["The right to establish and maintain institutions for religious and charitable purposes, manage its own affairs in religious matters, own and acquire movable and immovable property, and administer such property in accordance with the law."]
26.0.Chunk Text: ["The right to establish and maintain institutions for religious and charitable purposes, manage its own affairs in religious matters, own and acquire movable and immovable property, and administer such property in accordance with the law."]

27.0.Title: Freedom to pay taxes for promoting any particular religion.
27.0.Description: ["No person shall be compelled to pay any taxes whose proceeds are specifically appropriated to pay for the promotion or maintenance of any particular religion or religious denomination."]
27.0.Chunk Text: ["No person shall be compelled to pay any taxes whose proceeds are specifically appropriated to pay for the promotion or maintenance of any particular religion or religious denomination."]

28.0.Title: Freedom to attend religious instruction or worship at certain educational institutions.
28.0.Description: ["(1) No religious instruction shall be provided in any educational institution wholly maintained out of State funds. (", "2) Nothing in clause (1) shall apply to an educational institution administered by the State but established under an endowment or trust requiring religious instruction. (", "3) No person attending an educational institution recognised by the State or receiving State funding shall be required to participate in religious instruction or attend religious worship unless consent has been given by the individual or their guardian."]
28.0.Chunk Text: ["(1) No religious instruction shall be provided in any educational institution wholly maintained out of State funds.(2) Nothing in clause (1) shall apply to an educational institution administered by the State but established under an endowment or trust requiring religious instruction.(3) No person attending an educational institution recognised by the State or receiving State funding shall be required to participate in religious instruction or attend religious worship unless consent has been given by the individual or their guardian."]

29.0.Title: Protection of interests of minorities
29.0.Description: ["(1) Any section of the citizens residing in India or any part thereof, having a distinct language, script, or culture of its own, shall have the right to conserve it. (", "2) No citizen shall be denied admission into any educational institution maintained by the State or receiving aid from State funds on grounds only of religion, race, caste, language, or any of them."]
29.0.Chunk Text: ["(1) Any section of the citizens residing in India or any part thereof, having a distinct language, script, or culture of its own, shall have the right to conserve it.(2) No citizen shall be denied admission into any educational institution maintained by the State or receiving aid from State funds on grounds only of religion, race, caste, language, or any of them."]

30.0.Title: Right of minorities to establish and administer educational institutions
30.0.Description: ["All minorities, regardless of religion or language, have the right to establish and run their own schools.", "The State must ensure that any law requiring the acquisition of property by a minority school doesn\u2019t restrict or abolish the right guaranteed in clause (1).", "The State must also ensure that it doesn\u2019t discriminate against any school based on whether it\u2019s run by a minority, regardless of religion or language."]
30.0.Chunk Text: ["All minorities, regardless of religion or language, have the right to establish and run their own schools.", "The State must ensure that any law requiring the acquisition of property by a minority school doesn\u2019t restrict or abolish the right guaranteed in clause (1).The State must also ensure that it doesn\u2019t discriminate against any school based on whether it\u2019s run by a minority, regardless of religion or language."]

31.0.Title: Right to property
31.0.Description: ["No person shall be deprived of property except by law, and compensation is provided for acquisitions."]
31.0.Chunk Text: ["No person shall be deprived of property except by authority of law.", "No property movable or immovable including any interest in any company owning any commercial or industrial undertaking shall be taken possession of or acquired for public purposes unless the law provides for compensation and specifies principles for determination. [", "Omitted by 44th Amendment 1979]."]

32.0.Title: Remedies for Enforcement of Rights
32.0.Description: ["Guarantees right to approach Supreme Court for enforcement of fundamental rights via writs."]
32.0.Chunk Text: ["(1) The right to move the Supreme Court by appropriate proceedings for the enforcement of the rights conferred by this Part is guaranteed.", "(2) The Supreme Court shall have power to issue directions or orders or writs including habeas corpus mandamus prohibition quo warranto and certiorari for enforcement of rights.", "(3) Parliament may empower other courts to exercise such powers.", "(4) The right guaranteed by this article shall not be suspended except as provided by the Constitution."]
